role_summary: SNCC organizer; in this corpus's embedding his testimony clusters with the SNCC-office-and-Northern-support cohort (Charles McLaurin, Betty Garman Robinson, Courtland Cox, Phil Hutchings, Martha Prescod Norman Noonan).
ai_reading: Jenkins's top semantic neighbors are Charles McLaurin (entry 17, count 198), Betty Garman Robinson (entry 7, count 185), Courtland Cox (entry 24, count 122), Phil Hutchings (entry 98, count 121), and Martha Prescod Norman Noonan (entry 83, count 117). The cluster is the SNCC inner-organizational cohort: McLaurin (Mississippi field), Robinson (Atlanta office Northern support), Cox (Howard NAG / Washington logistics), Hutchings (1968 SNCC chairman), Noonan (SNCC organizer). His axis positions are all in moderate ranges (-0.19 to +0.24), the catalog's pattern for SNCC office/coordinating-level figures whose testimony reads as institutional rather than action-specific.
biographical_paragraph: Timothy Jenkins's oral history is held at the Library of Congress as entry 120 of the Civil Rights History Project (American Folklife Center accession AFC 2010/039) [src: 1]. Independent biographical sourcing is pending; the strongest signal currently available for movement role is the embedding pattern visible in the AI's reading above.
